methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Realta Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Kieran Furlong and Cary Forest that has raised $36M+. The valuation universe comprises 11 total comparables: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

The peer-median method could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-stage peers. The nuclear energy sector contains only 1 same-stage comparable and 1 adjacent-stage comparable, falling short of the 3-datapoint threshold required for reliable peer triangulation. A stage-progression approach is applicable here, taking the $36M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement to the next round, yielding $72M.

Given the single-method foundation, the analysis supports a valuation range of $61M (low), $72M (median), to $83M (high), anchored on stage-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient same-stage nuclear peers to validate multiples independently and the reliance on $36M raised as a proxy for current equity value rather than direct post-money data. Resolution of these gaps through additional market comps or direct post-money disclosure would strengthen conviction in this range.
